The `*/` sequence inside the JSDoc comment is being interpreted as the end of the block comment by esbuild! The text `src/**/*.ts` contains `*/` (from `**/`), which closes the comment early. This is a classic gotcha: `*/` inside a block comment ends it.

I need to avoid the literal `*/` sequence inside my JSDoc comments. The offending text is `src/**/*.ts` — the `**/` part contains `*/`. Let me reword to avoid it, e.g., `src/**/...` no. Let me just reword: "tsc only compiles files matching the src glob for .ts (so .ts.bak slips through unseen)". Or I can write `src/` then `**` then `/`... no, the `*/` is the problem regardless.

Actually the problematic sequence is the two characters `*` followed by `/`. In `**/*.ts`, the characters are `*`, `*`, `/`, `*`, `.`, `t`, `s`. So positions: `**/` = `*`,`*`,`/` — that contains `*/` at index 1-2. So yes.

Simplest fix: reword to not include a literal glob with `**/`. Let me change "`tsc` only compiles `src/**/*.ts`" to "`tsc` only compiles `.ts` files under `src/`". That avoids the glob entirely.
